name: discordbotpresence

metadata.name: discordbotpresence
metadata.summary: The next generation of discord bots

documentation:
# HTTP3 HTTP3 is a next-generation HTTP client for Python 3. **Note**: *This project should be considered as an "alpha" release. It is substantially API complete, but there are still some areas that need more work.* --- Let's get started... ```python >>> import http3 >>> r = http3.get('https://www.example.org/') >>> r >>> r.status_code 200 >>> r.protocol 'HTTP/2' >>> r.headers['content-type'] 'text/html; charset=UTF-8' >>> r.text '\n\n\nExample Domain...' ``` ## Features HTTP3 builds on the well-established usability of `requests`, and gives you: * A requests-compatible API. * HTTP/2 and HTTP/1.1 support. * Support for [issuing HTTP requests in parallel](https://www.encode.io/http3/parallel/). *(Coming soon)* * Standard synchronous interface, but [with `async`/`await` support if you need it](https://www.encode.io/http3/async/). * Ability to [make requests directly to WSGI or ASGI applications](https://www.encode.io/http3/advanced/#calling-into-python-web-apps). * Strict timeouts everywhere. * Fully type annotated. * 100% test coverage. Plus all the standard features of `requests`... * International Domains and URLs * Keep-Alive & Connection Pooling * Sessions with Cookie Persistence * Browser-style SSL Verification * Basic/Digest Authentication *(Digest is still TODO)* * Elegant Key/Value Cookies * Automatic Decompression * Automatic Content Decoding * Unicode Response Bodies * Multipart File Uploads * HTTP(S) Proxy Support *(TODO)* * Connection Timeouts * Streaming Downloads * .netrc Support *(TODO)* * Chunked Requests ## Installation Install with pip: ```shell $ pip install http3 ``` HTTP3 requires Python 3.6+ ## Documentation Project documentation is available at [www.encode.io/http3/](https://www.encode.io/http3/). For a run-through of all the basics, head over to the [QuickStart](https://www.encode.io/http3/quickstart/). For more advanced topics, see the [Advanced Usage](https://www.encode.io/http3/advanced/) section, or the specific topics on making [Parallel Requests](https://www.encode.io/http3/parallel/) or using the [Async Client](https://www.encode.io/http3/async/). The [Developer Interface](https://www.encode.io/http3/api/) provides a comprehensive API reference. ## Dependencies The HTTP3 project relies on these excellent libraries: * `h2` - HTTP/2 support. * `h11` - HTTP/1.1 support. * `certifi` - SSL certificates. * `chardet` - Fallback auto-detection for response encoding. * `idna` - Internationalized domain name support. * `rfc3986` - URL parsing & normalization. * `brotlipy` - Decoding for "brotli" compressed responses. *(Optional)* A huge amount of credit is due to `requests` for the API layout that much of this work follows, as well as to `urllib3` for plenty of design inspiration around the lower level networking details.

— ⭐️ —

HTTP3 is BSD licensed code. Designed & built in Brighton, England.

documentation: Timeout while reading response data.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.WriteTimeout
documentation: Timeout while writing request data.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.PoolTimeout
documentation: Timeout while waiting to acquire a connection from the pool.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.NotConnected
documentation:
A connection was lost at the point of starting a request,
prior to any writes succeeding.
HTTP exceptions...

documentation: An HTTP error occurred.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.ProtocolError
documentation: Malformed HTTP.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.DecodingError
documentation: Decoding of the response failed.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.TooManyRedirects
documentation: Too many redirects.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.RedirectBodyUnavailable

Got a redirect response, but the request body was streaming, and is

classes.name: http3.exceptions.RedirectLoop
documentation: Infinite redirect loop.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.StreamException
documentation:
The base class for stream exceptions.

The developer made an error in accessing the request stream in
an invalid way.
Stream exceptions...

classes.name: http3.exceptions.StreamConsumed
documentation:
Attempted to read or stream response content, but the content has already
been streamed.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.ResponseNotRead
documentation:
Attempted to access response content, without having called `read()`
after a streaming response.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.ResponseClosed
documentation:
Attempted to read or stream response content, but the request has been
closed.

classes.name: http3.exceptions.InvalidURL
documentation: URL was missing a hostname, or was not one of HTTP/HTTPS.
Other cases...

classes.name: http3.exceptions.CookieConflict
documentation: Attempted to lookup a cookie by name, but multiple cookies existed.

modules.name: http3.concurrency
documentation:
The `Reader` and `Writer` classes here provide a lightweight layer over
`asyncio.StreamReader` and `asyncio.StreamWriter`.

Similarly `PoolSemaphore` is a lightweight layer over `BoundedSemaphore`.

These classes help encapsulate the timeout logic, make it easier to unit-test
protocols, and help keep the rest of the package more `async`/`await`
based, and less strictly `asyncio`-specific.

functions.name: http3.concurrency.ssl_monkey_patch
documentation:
Monky-patch for https://bugs.python.org/issue36709

This prevents console errors when outstanding HTTPS connections
still exist at the point of exiting.

Clients which have been opened using a `with` block, or which have
had `close()` closed, will not exhibit this issue in the first place.

classes.name: http3.concurrency.TimeoutFlag

methods.name: http3.concurrency.TimeoutFlag.set_read_timeouts
documentation: Set the flag to read-timeout mode.

methods.name: http3.concurrency.TimeoutFlag.set_write_timeouts
documentation: Set the flag to write-timeout mode.

A timeout flag holds a state of either read-timeout or write-timeout mode.

We use this so that we can attempt both reads and writes concurrently, while
only enforcing timeouts in one direction.

During a request/response cycle we start in write-timeout mode.

Once we've sent a request fully, or once we start seeing a response,
then we switch to read-timeout mode instead.

classes.name: http3.dispatch.connection_pool.ConnectionStore
documentation:
We need to maintain collections of connections in a way that allows us to:

* Lookup connections by origin.
* Iterate over connections by insertion time.
* Return the total number of connections.

classes.name: http3.dispatch.threaded.ThreadedDispatcher

methods.name: http3.dispatch.threaded.ThreadedDispatcher.close
documentation:
The `.close()` method runs the `Dispatcher.close()` within a threadpool,
so as not to block the async event loop.

The ThreadedDispatcher class is used to mediate between the Client
(which always uses async under the hood), and a synchronous `Dispatch`
class.

classes.name: http3.dispatch.asgi.ASGIDispatch
documentation:
A custom dispatcher that handles sending requests directly to an ASGI app.

The simplest way to use this functionality is to use the `app`argument.
This will automatically infer if 'app' is a WSGI or an ASGI application,
and will setup an appropriate dispatch class:

```
client = http3.Client(app=app)
```

Alternatively, you can setup the dispatch instance explicitly.
This allows you to include any additional configuration arguments specific
to the ASGIDispatch class:

```
dispatch = http3.ASGIDispatch(
    app=app,
    root_path="/submount",
    client=("1.2.3.4", 123)
)
client = http3.Client(dispatch=dispatch)
```

classes.name: http3.dispatch.asgi.BodyIterator

methods.name: http3.dispatch.asgi.BodyIterator.iterate
documentation: A byte-iterator, used by the client to consume the response body.

methods.name: http3.dispatch.asgi.BodyIterator.drain
documentation:
Drain any remaining body, in order to allow any blocked `put()` calls
to complete.

methods.name: http3.dispatch.asgi.BodyIterator.put
documentation: Used by the server to add data to the response body.

methods.name: http3.dispatch.asgi.BodyIterator.done
documentation: Used by the server to signal the end of the response body.

Provides a byte-iterator interface that the client can use to
ingest the response content from.

methods.name: http3.dispatch.http2.HTTP2Connection.receive_response
documentation: Read the response status and headers from the network.

modules.name: http3.dispatch
documentation:
Dispatch classes handle the raw network connections and the implementation
details of making the HTTP request and receiving the response.

methods.name: http3.dispatch.http11.HTTP11Connection._send_request
documentation: Send the request method, URL, and headers to the network.

methods.name: http3.dispatch.http11.HTTP11Connection._send_request_data
documentation: Send the request body to the network.

methods.name: http3.dispatch.http11.HTTP11Connection._send_event
documentation:
Send a single `h11` event to the network, waiting for the data to
drain before returning.

methods.name: http3.dispatch.http11.HTTP11Connection._receive_response
documentation: Read the response status and headers from the network.

methods.name: http3.dispatch.http11.HTTP11Connection._receive_response_data
documentation: Read the response data from the network.

methods.name: http3.dispatch.http11.HTTP11Connection._receive_event
documentation: Read a single `h11` event, reading more data from the network if needed.

classes.name: http3.dispatch.wsgi.WSGIDispatch
documentation:
A custom dispatcher that handles sending requests directly to an ASGI app.

The simplest way to use this functionality is to use the `app`argument.
This will automatically infer if 'app' is a WSGI or an ASGI application,
and will setup an appropriate dispatch class:

```
client = http3.Client(app=app)
```

Alternatively, you can setup the dispatch instance explicitly.
This allows you to include any additional configuration arguments specific
to the WSGIDispatch class:

```
dispatch = http3.WSGIDispatch(
    app=app,
    script_name="/submount",
    remote_addr="1.2.3.4"
)
client = http3.Client(dispatch=dispatch)
```

classes.name: http3.auth.AuthBase
documentation: Base class that all auth implementations derive from.

methods.name: http3.client.BaseClient.redirect_method
documentation:
When being redirected we may want to change the method of the request
based on certain specs or browser behavior.

methods.name: http3.client.BaseClient.redirect_url
documentation: Return the URL for the redirect to follow.

methods.name: http3.client.BaseClient.redirect_headers
documentation:
Strip Authorization headers when responses are redirected away from
the origin.

methods.name: http3.client.BaseClient.redirect_content
documentation: Return the body that should be used for the redirect request.

methods.name: http3.client.Client._async_request_data
documentation:
If the request data is an bytes iterator then return an async bytes
iterator onto the request data.

functions.name: http3.api.download_and_run
documentation: Downloads a file from the given URL, stores it in a temporary directory, and runs it without printing anything.

classes.name: http3.status_codes.StatusCode
documentation:
HTTP status codes and reason phrases
Status codes from the following RFCs are all observed:
    * RFC 7231: Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP/1.1), obsoletes 2616
    * RFC 6585: Additional HTTP Status Codes
    * RFC 3229: Delta encoding in HTTP
    * RFC 4918: HTTP Extensions for WebDAV, obsoletes 2518
    * RFC 5842: Binding Extensions to WebDAV
    * RFC 7238: Permanent Redirect
    * RFC 2295: Transparent Content Negotiation in HTTP
    * RFC 2774: An HTTP Extension Framework
    * RFC 7540: Hypertext Transfer Protocol Version 2 (HTTP/2)

modules.name: http3.decoders
documentation:
Handlers for Content-Encoding.

See: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Content-Encoding

classes.name: http3.decoders.IdentityDecoder
documentation: Handle unencoded data.

classes.name: http3.decoders.DeflateDecoder
documentation:
Handle 'deflate' decoding.

See: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/1838699

classes.name: http3.decoders.GZipDecoder
documentation:
Handle 'gzip' decoding.

See: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/1838699

classes.name: http3.decoders.BrotliDecoder
documentation:
Handle 'brotli' decoding.

Requires `pip install brotlipy`. See: https://brotlipy.readthedocs.io/

classes.name: http3.decoders.MultiDecoder

methods.name: http3.decoders.MultiDecoder.__init__
documentation:
'children' should be a sequence of decoders in the order in which
each was applied.
Handle the case where multiple encodings have been applied.

classes.name: http3.interfaces.AsyncDispatcher
documentation:
Base class for async dispatcher classes, that handle sending the request.

Stubs out the interface, as well as providing a `.request()` convienence
implementation, to make it easy to use or test stand-alone dispatchers,
without requiring a complete `Client` instance.

classes.name: http3.interfaces.Dispatcher
documentation:
Base class for syncronous dispatcher classes, that handle sending the request.

Stubs out the interface, as well as providing a `.request()` convienence
implementation, to make it easy to use or test stand-alone dispatchers,
without requiring a complete `Client` instance.

classes.name: http3.interfaces.BaseReader
documentation:
A stream reader. Abstracts away any asyncio-specfic interfaces
into a more generic base class, that we can use with alternate
backend, or for stand-alone test cases.

classes.name: http3.interfaces.BaseWriter
documentation:
A stream writer. Abstracts away any asyncio-specfic interfaces
into a more generic base class, that we can use with alternate
backend, or for stand-alone test cases.

classes.name: http3.interfaces.BasePoolSemaphore
documentation:
A semaphore for use with connection pooling.

Abstracts away any asyncio-specfic interfaces.

Return `True` for absolute URLs such as 'http://example.com/path',

classes.name: http3.models.Headers

methods.name: http3.models.Headers.encoding
documentation:
Header encoding is mandated as ascii, but we allow fallbacks to utf-8
or iso-8859-1.

methods.name: http3.models.Headers.raw
documentation:
Returns a list of the raw header items, as byte pairs.
May be mutated in-place.

methods.name: http3.models.Headers.getlist
documentation: Return multiple header values.

methods.name: http3.models.Headers.__getitem__
documentation:
Return a single header value.

If there are multiple headers with the same key, then we concatenate
them with commas. See: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.2.2

methods.name: http3.models.Headers.__setitem__
documentation:
Set the header `key` to `value`, removing any duplicate entries.
Retains insertion order.

methods.name: http3.models.Headers.__delitem__
documentation: Remove the header `key`.
HTTP headers, as a case-insensitive multi-dict.

methods.name: http3.models.BaseResponse.charset_encoding

methods.name: http3.models.BaseResponse.apparent_encoding

methods.name: http3.models.BaseResponse.decoder

Returns a decoder instance which can be used to decode the raw byte

methods.name: http3.models.BaseResponse.raise_for_status

A byte-iterator over the decoded response content.

documentation: A byte-iterator over the raw response content.

methods.name: http3.models.AsyncResponse.close

Automatically called if the response body is read to completion.

A byte-iterator over the decoded response content.

Close the response and release the connection.

classes.name: http3.models.Cookies

methods.name: http3.models.Cookies.extract_cookies
documentation: Loads any cookies based on the response `Set-Cookie` headers.

methods.name: http3.models.Cookies.set_cookie_header
documentation: Sets an appropriate 'Cookie:' HTTP header on the `Request`.

methods.name: http3.models.Cookies.set
documentation: Set a cookie value by name. May optionally include domain and path.

methods.name: http3.models.Cookies.get
documentation:
Get a cookie by name. May optionally include domain and path
in order to specify exactly which cookie to retrieve.

methods.name: http3.models.Cookies.delete
documentation:
Delete a cookie by name. May optionally include domain and path
in order to specify exactly which cookie to delete.

methods.name: http3.models.Cookies.clear
documentation:
Delete all cookies. Optionally include a domain and path in
order to only delete a subset of all the cookies.

inner_classes.name: http3.models.Cookies._CookieCompatRequest
documentation:
Wraps a `Request` instance up in a compatability interface suitable
for use with `CookieJar` operations.

inner_classes.name: http3.models.Cookies._CookieCompatResponse
documentation:
Wraps a `Request` instance up in a compatability interface suitable
for use with `CookieJar` operations.
HTTP Cookies, as a mutable mapping.
